_meta.resume_note: Pinned to Merritt College's official archived 2025-2026 CurriQunet catalog. Preserve the dependency allowing an unused List A course to satisfy List B.

degrees.tmc_version_basis: The catalog identifies the program as the Associate in Arts for Transfer in Economics and prints the Economics transfer core and elective lists.
degrees.catalog_status_seen: Published in the official archived 2025-2026 catalog. CurriQunet labels the entire archived award presentation Historical, while several pre-CCN course rows also display Historical; this reflects the archived/current-status interface and needs human review rather than an inferred inactive award.

sources.locator: 2025-2026 archived catalog, Courses and Programs > Programs by Alphabetical Order > Economics - AA-T Degree > Requirements; Required Courses, Core Courses, List A, List B, and Major Requirement Units
Economics - AA-T Degree > Summary; 60 CSU-transferable semester units, Cal-GETC, residency, GPA, and major-grade requirements

major_groups.label_seen: List A — Select one course for 4-5 units
List B — Select one course for 4-5 units or any course in List A not already used
major_groups.choice_structure_seen: Select MATH 003C or one course from List A that was not already used to satisfy List A.

major_groups.label_seen: California General Education Transfer Curriculum (Cal-GETC) Requirements
CSU-transferable electives as needed to complete 60 units

degrees.notes: The flat first-pass schema cannot enforce that a List A course reused in List B must be a different course; the exact dependency is preserved in choice_structure_seen. The archived CurriQunet presentation marks ECON 001, ECON 002, BUS 001A, and BUS 001B as Historical while the 2025-2026 award itself is present; likely later common-course-number replacements should be checked during human validation.

notes: The official 2025-2026 associate-award table and alphabetical program index list one general Economics award, the AA-T, and no local Economics AA or AS.
